commands.review_summary:
The PR adds per-source-message media tracking for Telegram media groups and a regression test so album sibling messages hydrate to `media://inbound/...` paths in prompt context.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +105. Total +121 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main collects all album media but only maps `params.allMedia[0]` to the primary message id while cached sibling messages keep raw `telegram:file/...` refs. I did not run live Telegram in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is live Telegram proof and maintainer validation, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(telegram): hydrate album photo context
target.body:
Closes #96846

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Telegram users who send photo albums can leave non-primary album photos in the model-facing conversation context as raw `telegram:file/...` references instead of usable media paths.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Telegram media-group flush already downloads every album photo for the current turn, but prompt-context hydration only associated media with the primary message id. This change carries the resolved album media by source Telegram message id into prompt-context hydration so sibling album messages are hydrated to `media://inbound/...` paths without changing the existing current-turn media array, reply-chain media handling, or channel policy.

AI-assisted. I reviewed and own the diff. No transcript section is included because this heartbeat run could not ask for approval to attach sanitized local logs.

## User Impact

Telegram photo albums continue to process as one turn, and sibling album photos no longer appear to the model as unreadable Telegram file references in structured context. This should reduce missing-image behavior for multi-photo Telegram sends once live Telegram proof confirms the full channel path.

## Evidence

Local changed-path proof:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.downloads-media-file-path-no-file-download.e2e.test.ts --testNamePattern 'hydrates every media-group message' --reporter verbose --maxWorkers 1 --testTimeout 30000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.downloads-media-file-path-no-file-download.e2e.test.ts --reporter verbose --maxWorkers 1`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts --testNamePattern 'keeps direct Telegram media context|includes replied image media|hydrates reply chains|does not hydrate reply media|does not fetch reply media' --reporter verbose --maxWorkers 1`
- `git diff --check`
- `/Users/harjothk/.local/bin/python3.12 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

Proof pending: this is a draft-only PR because I did not run a live Telegram Bot API album. Needs a maintainer or agent with Telegram credentials to send a 3+ photo album and confirm all photos reach the model-facing turn/context, with private details redacted.
target.author: harjothkhara
